terms.definition: The force that pushes a rocket upward, created by burning fuel and pushing exhaust gas downward.
A substance that lets fuel burn even without air, needed because there's no oxygen in space.
A separate section of a rocket with its own engine and fuel, which falls away once used.
The cargo a rocket carries, like a satellite, science experiment, or camera.
The electronics that control and monitor a rocket during flight.
The system that steers a rocket to keep it on the right path.
The highest point a rocket reaches during its flight.
Parachutes or streamers that slow a rocket down so it lands safely.
A flat surface near the bottom of a rocket that keeps it flying straight.
The pointed top part of a rocket that cuts through the air.
The moment a rocket motor or engine is lit and starts producing thrust.
A straight guide rail that helps a rocket go straight during the first moments of flight.
